machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin (forms the OM channel of the basal body outer ring)
machinery_genes.quote: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier 2003 (EPEC E2348/69). EscC = SctC/HrcC family secretin. Ogino 2006 (PMC1447006) independently shows 'EscC was a major protein component of the outer ring in the core basal body.' Same esc gene present in the O157 LEE (escC, formerly sepC; Perna 1998).

machinery_genes.role: inner-ring lipoprotein (links inner and outer membrane rings of the basal body)
machinery_genes.quote: a PrgK orthologue, EscJ, is thought to form a portion of the inner ring
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EscJ = SctJ/PrgK family inner-ring lipoprotein; escJ (formerly sepD) listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ring component of the basal body
machinery_genes.quote: EscD is predicted to be a 45-kDa protein and shows amino acid sequence similarity to Yersinia YscD, a bacterial inner membrane protein
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EscD = SctD/YscD family inner-membrane ring. escD listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.role: needle subunit (polymerises to form the injectisome needle)
machinery_genes.quote: EPEC EscF is predicted to be an 8-kDa protein and may polymerize to form the thin needle in the needle structure
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EscF = SctF/YscF/MxiH needle subunit. escF listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic secretion ATPase (energises the apparatus / strips chaperones)
machinery_genes.quote: The system contains a single ATPase, EscN, which is essential for uncoupling of proteins from their complexes with chaperones before the delivery.
machinery_genes.note: Bzdzion 2016 (EPEC). EscN = SctN/YscN/InvC ATPase. Gauthier 2003 also names 'EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase'. escN (formerly sepB) listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.quote: We present the 3.3 A resolution cryo-EM structure of the enteropathogenic Escherichia coli ATPase EscN in complex with its central stalk EscO.
machinery_genes.note: Majewski 2019 (EPEC). EscO = SctO/YscO/InvI ATPase stalk. Lower-confidence as a LEE-locus call: EscO/Orf15 is part of the LEE export apparatus but was not separately tabulated under the 'Type III secretion apparatus' description in Perna 1998; included on functional naming + EPEC LEE provenance.

machinery_genes.quote: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier 2003 (EPEC). EscV = SctV/YscV/InvA/LcrD export-apparatus ring. escV (formerly sepA) listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.quote: several Esc proteins, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, and EscU, are predicted to be components of the basal body
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EscR = SctR/YscR/SpaP export-gate. escR (formerly sepI) listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.quote: several Esc proteins, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, and EscU, are predicted to be components of the basal body
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EscS = SctS/YscS/SpaQ export-gate. escS (formerly sepH) listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.quote: several Esc proteins, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, and EscU, are predicted to be components of the basal body
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EscT = SctT/YscT/SpaR export-gate. escT (formerly sepG) listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.quote: several Esc proteins, namely, EscR, EscS, EscT, and EscU, are predicted to be components of the basal body
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EscU = SctU/YscU/SpaS autoprotease switch. escU (formerly sepF) listed as 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 LEE (Perna 1998, Table 2).

machinery_genes.quote: The esc and sep genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: Perna 1998, complete O157:H7 (EDL933) LEE. sepQ tabulated with the protein description 'Type III secretion apparatus' (Table 2). SepQ = SctQ/YscQ/SpaO C-ring/sorting-platform family (EscQ in later nomenclature).

machinery_genes.role: type III secretion apparatus component (inner-membrane; later renamed EscZ/Orf3)
machinery_genes.quote: The esc and sep genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: Perna 1998, O157:H7 LEE. sepZ tabulated as 'Type III secretion apparatus' (Table 2). Listed as machinery, not effector; lower-confidence family assignment (sometimes annotated EscZ).

machinery_genes.role: EspA filament/sheath that extends the needle and forms the translocation conduit to the host membrane
machinery_genes.quote: we previously identified a novel needle complex (NC) composed of a basal body and a needle structure containing an expandable EspA sheath-like structure as a central part of the EPEC TTS apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006 (EPEC). EspA forms the hollow filamentous sheath extending the EscF needle; part of the translocation apparatus (not a translocated effector). espA present in the O157 LEE (LEE4/sep operon, Perna 1998).

machinery_genes.quote: The esc and sep genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: EspB and EspD form the host-membrane translocation pore (translocon) at the tip of the EspA filament; per the apparatus brief, translocon proteins count as machinery, not effectors. espD is in the LEE4 operon of the O157 LEE (Perna 1998). Quote here supports the esc/sep apparatus locus; EspB/EspD translocon role is the established consensus for this system.

notes: System: E. coli O157:H7 Sakai has a SINGLE type III injectisome, encoded by the LEE (locus of enterocyte effacement) pathogenicity island and built from the esc/sep gene set (the Esc/Sep injectisome, Sct nomenclature). Critical anti-circularity point for this instance: prophage Sp4 (and the other lambdoid prophages) encode T3SS EFFECTORS only, NOT secretion machinery. All Sakai T3SS effectors, including the prophage-borne non-LEE effectors curated for this instance (ECs0847/ECs0848/ECs0850), are translocated by the one LEE-encoded apparatus (Tobe et al 2006, PNAS, DOI 10.1073/pnas.0604891103: 'the majority of functional effector genes are encoded by nine exchangeable effector loci that lie within lambdoid prophages'). Therefore the apparatus answer key for this instance is the LEE Esc/Sep injectisome, NOT genes inside Sp4. Apparatus paper provenance: the gene-by-gene apparatus list is taken from the complete O157:H7 (EDL933) LEE sequence (Perna et al 1998, Infect Immun), whose esc/sep apparatus genes are >95% identical and syntenic to the Sakai LEE; functional naming quotes for individual components come from EPEC E2348/69 studies (Ogino 2006; Gauthier 2003; Bzdzion 2016; Majewski 2019), the archetypal LEE T3SS, transferable to O157 because the apparatus genes are essentially identical across LEE strains. Genes listed (Sct family): EscC (secretin SctC), EscJ (inner ring SctJ), EscD (IM ring SctD), EscR/EscS/EscT/EscU (export gate SctR/S/T/U), EscV (export-apparatus ring SctV), EscN (ATPase SctN), EscO (ATPase stalk SctO), SepQ (C-ring/sorting platform SctQ), SepZ (LEE apparatus, later EscZ), plus translocation-apparatus proteins EspA (needle-extension sheath) and EspD (translocon pore). Lower-confidence calls: SepZ (family annotation uncertain), EscO (LEE export-apparatus protein not separately tabulated under the apparatus description in Perna 1998), and EspD (cited via the esc/sep apparatus statement rather than a per-gene quote; included because the brief classes translocon proteins as machinery). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors (Tir, Map, EspF, EspG, EspH, EspZ and all Nle/prophage effectors incl. ECs0847-0850); translocated chaperones (CesT, CesD, CesAB/CesA); the translocated intimin receptor pathway protein intimin/Eae (adhesin, not injectisome); transcriptional regulators (Ler, GrlA/GrlR). Note: the prompt's 'prophage_Sp4' label vs Tobe 2006's prophage map: in Tobe et al, ECs0846-0850 (NleB2-1, NleC, NleH1-1, NleD) map to the Sp3 prophage region while Sp4 is annotated with EspF2-1'/EspV' (ECs1126/1127); this is a prophage-label/numbering discrepancy in the source genome annotation and does not change the machinery answer, since all these effectors share the one LEE injectisome. All quotes verbatim from the cited PubMed/PMC sources (DOIs and PMIDs verified via get_article_metadata).
